nodes.name: Eastern long-tailed hornbill
nodes.summary: The eastern long-tailed hornbill (Horizocerus cassini) is a species of bird in the hornbill family Bucerotidae found in humid forests of West Africa. It was formerly considered to be conspecific with the western long-tailed hornbill (Horizocerus albocristatus) with the English name "white-crested hornbill". Taxonomy The eastern long-tailed hornbill was formally described in 1903 by the German naturalist Otto Finsch based on a specimen from Gabon in West Africa. He coined the binomial name Ortholophus cassini. The specific epithet was chosen to honour the American ornithologist John Cassin. The species is now placed in the genus Horizocerus that was introduced in 1899 by the American ornithologist Harry C. Oberholser. The eastern long-tailed hornbill was formerly considered to be conspecific with the western long-tailed hornbill (Horizocerus albocristatus) with the English name "white-crested hornbill". References

nodes.summary: Horizocerus（Horizocerus）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联4种鸟类，如Western long-tailed hornbill、Eastern long-tailed hornbill、Western dwarf hornbill等4种鸟类。

nodes.summary: Eastern long-tailed hornbill（Horizocerus cassini）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如Eastern long-tailed hornbill。
